regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an acute multi-front crisis as the April 22 ceasefire expires with the US deploying a third carrier strike group and 10,000+ troops, while Tehran simultaneously confronts internal instability marked by protest crackdowns, security force defections, and accelerating economic collapse from sanctions and Hormuz blockade pressures. Iran has signaled military escalation options through advanced missile unveilings and Strait of Hormuz provocations, while rejecting diplomatic frameworks and warning of 'savage aggression' response.
regime_fall.last_major_event: The US-Iran ceasefire expired on April 22, 2026, with the US deploying a third aircraft carrier strike group (USS George HW Bush) and 10,000+ additional troops to the Middle East, while simultaneously halting $500 million in dollar shipments to Iraq and freezing security cooperation to counter Iran-backed militias, as Iran unveiled advanced missile capabilities and warned of military escalation.
